{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}} Aza Baba 31 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 55|緯度秒 = 37.799 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =8|経度秒 = 51.648 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Inugashira Reishin |社格 = former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}}]] [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Russo-Japanese War monument in the precincts]] '''Kasumekentou Shrine''' (かすめけんとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of 100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites]. === Overview === It is regarded as one of the six shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9-10 of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}), it was built enshrining {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}. It was formerly in Nishikasume Morisaki of neighboring Kamiwada village and was therefore called Kasume Shrine, but repeated flooding moved it to the current location. In {{ill|lt=Eien|Q1324864|WD=Q1324864|1=Eien}} 1 ({{ill|lt=987|Q23644|WD=Q23644|1=987}}), Kumano Sansho Daigongen was jointly enshrined. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Kanno|Q1145053|WD=Q1145053|1=Kannō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1350|Q6094|WD=Q6094|1=1350}}), {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} donated 100 kanmon as land for Kumano Gongen. The Okazaki domain lord {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}} protected this shrine and rebuilt the honden in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}}). In Keichō 10 ({{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}}), he dedicated a torii made of {{ill|lt=Shakudani stone|Q11599510|WD=Q11599510|1=Shakudani Stone}} from Echizen[http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/41mutsumiseibu.pdf Mutsumi West School District | Okazaki Town Stories District Page | Okazaki City 100th Anniversary Site]. A red-seal grant from {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, dated August 28, Keichō 8 ({{ill|lt=1603|Q6735|WD=Q6735|1=1603}}), bestowed 43 koku of shrine lands. The same document says "Inugashira Shrine lands," and a red-seal grant of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Hidetada|Q314464|WD=Q314464|1=Tokugawa Hidetada}} in {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1617|Q6798|WD=Q6798|1=1617}}) also says "Inugashira lands," showing that in the early modern period it was publicly called Inugashira Shrine. A munafuda for construction of the haiden in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1793|Q7807|WD=Q7807|1=1793}}) has been confirmed with the inscription "Shikinaisha Kasume Shrine Inugashira Daimyōjin"Mutsumi Village Survey Association (ed.), ''Mutsumi Village History'', Nisshindō Shoten, 1926.. It appears to have started claiming identity as the shikinaisha Kasume Shrine after the late 18th century. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), by order of {{ill|lt=Nukata Prefecture|Q3070843|WD=Q3070843|1=Nukata Prefecture}}, it was renamed Kasumekentou Shrine. On September 18 of Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a gōsha. The honden was rebuilt in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}), and the haiden in Meiji 34 ({{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}}). This shrine is the starting point of the Tsutsumidōri Teyaga Otaōgi Festival{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou6.pdf |title=Okazaki City Historical Landscape Maintenance and Improvement Plan - Chapter 2 Historical landscape seen in rice-cultivation rites of the Mutsumi district |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |accessdate=2018-4-1 }}. Children's sumo is held at the autumn grand festival. === Cultural properties === The following properties are designated cultural properties of the city{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=List of Okazaki City Designated Cultural Properties | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. {| class="wikitable" ! Designated name !! Type !! Period !! Date designated |- | Inugashira Shrine stone torii || Structure || {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 10) || {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone karaneko || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || 1605 (Keichō 10) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone komainu || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keichō 15) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: Take the Nishio-bound bus via Shimo-Aono, get off at "Inai Nōkyō-mae," then about 800 m north. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition: Okazaki City History, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Okazaki City History Editorial Committee |page = 122 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 172-173 |isbn = }} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}} Aza Baba 31 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 55|緯度秒 = 37.799 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =8|経度秒 = 51.648 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Inugashira Reishin |社格 = former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}}]] [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Russo-Japanese War monument in the precincts]] '''Kasumekentou Shrine''' (かすめけんとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of 100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites]. === Overview === It is regarded as one of the six shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9-10 of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}), it was built enshrining {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}. It was formerly in Nishikasume Morisaki of neighboring Kamiwada village and was therefore called Kasume Shrine, but repeated flooding moved it to the current location. In {{ill|lt=Eien|Q1324864|WD=Q1324864|1=Eien}} 1 ({{ill|lt=987|Q23644|WD=Q23644|1=987}}), Kumano Sansho Daigongen was jointly enshrined. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Kanno|Q1145053|WD=Q1145053|1=Kannō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1350|Q6094|WD=Q6094|1=1350}}), {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} donated 100 kanmon as land for Kumano Gongen. The Okazaki domain lord {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}} protected this shrine and rebuilt the honden in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}}). In Keichō 10 ({{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}}), he dedicated a torii made of {{ill|lt=Shakudani stone|Q11599510|WD=Q11599510|1=Shakudani Stone}} from Echizen[http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/41mutsumiseibu.pdf Mutsumi West School District | Okazaki Town Stories District Page | Okazaki City 100th Anniversary Site]. A red-seal grant from {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, dated August 28, Keichō 8 ({{ill|lt=1603|Q6735|WD=Q6735|1=1603}}), bestowed 43 koku of shrine lands. The same document says "Inugashira Shrine lands," and a red-seal grant of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Hidetada|Q314464|WD=Q314464|1=Tokugawa Hidetada}} in {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1617|Q6798|WD=Q6798|1=1617}}) also says "Inugashira lands," showing that in the early modern period it was publicly called Inugashira Shrine. A munafuda for construction of the haiden in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1793|Q7807|WD=Q7807|1=1793}}) has been confirmed with the inscription "Shikinaisha Kasume Shrine Inugashira Daimyōjin"Mutsumi Village Survey Association (ed.), ''Mutsumi Village History'', Nisshindō Shoten, 1926.. It appears to have started claiming identity as the shikinaisha Kasume Shrine after the late 18th century. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), by order of {{ill|lt=Nukata Prefecture|Q3070843|WD=Q3070843|1=Nukata Prefecture}}, it was renamed Kasumekentou Shrine. On September 18 of Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a gōsha. The honden was rebuilt in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}), and the haiden in Meiji 34 ({{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}}). This shrine is the starting point of the Tsutsumidōri Teyaga Otaōgi Festival{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou6.pdf |title=Okazaki City Historical Landscape Maintenance and Improvement Plan - Chapter 2 Historical landscape seen in rice-cultivation rites of the Mutsumi district |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |accessdate=2018-4-1 }}. Children's sumo is held at the autumn grand festival. === Cultural properties === The following properties are designated cultural properties of the city{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=List of Okazaki City Designated Cultural Properties | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. {| class="wikitable" ! Designated name !! Type !! Period !! Date designated |- | Inugashira Shrine stone torii || Structure || {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 10) || {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|WD=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone karaneko || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || 1605 (Keichō 10) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone komainu || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|WD=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keichō 15) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: Take the Nishio-bound bus via Shimo-Aono, get off at "Inai Nōkyō-mae," then about 800 m north. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition: Okazaki City History, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Okazaki City History Editorial Committee |page = 122 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 172-173 |isbn = }} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|WD=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:pages]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}} Aza Baba 31 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 55|緯度秒 = 37.799 |経度度 = 137|経度分 =8|経度秒 = 51.648 |位置 = |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}, {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, Inugashira Reishin |社格 = former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}) |本殿 = |例祭 = |地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Torii dedicated by {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}}]] [[File:Okazaki-Kasumekento-Jinja-3.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Russo-Japanese War monument in the precincts]] '''Kasumekentou Shrine''' (かすめけんとうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Miyaji-chō (Okazaki)|Q107420129|WD=Q107420129|lt=Miyaji-chō|1=Miyaji-chō, Okazaki}}, {{ill|lt=Okazaki|Q242783|WD=Q242783|1=Okazaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is selected as one of the {{ill|lt=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites|Q11472991|WD=Q11472991|1=100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites}}[http://www.city.okazaki.aichi.jp/350/351/p006883_d/fil/1.pdf List of 100 Brilliant Okazaki Tourist Sites]. === Overview === It is regarded as one of the six shikinaisha in {{ill|lt=Hekikai District|Q5368803|WD=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} listed in volumes 9-10 of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. In {{ill|lt=Taihō|Q1346269|WD=Q1346269|lt=Taihō|1=Taihō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=701|Q23496|WD=Q23496|1=701}}), it was built enshrining {{ill|lt=Hoori|Q1051960|WD=Q1051960|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|1=Hoori}}. It was formerly in Nishikasume Morisaki of neighboring Kamiwada village and was therefore called Kasume Shrine, but repeated flooding moved it to the current location. In {{ill|lt=Eien|Q1324864|WD=Q1324864|1=Eien}} 1 ({{ill|lt=987|Q23644|WD=Q23644|1=987}}), Kumano Sansho Daigongen was jointly enshrined. It is said that in {{ill|lt=Kanno|Q1145053|WD=Q1145053|1=Kannō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1350|Q6094|WD=Q6094|1=1350}}), {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|WD=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}} donated 100 kanmon as land for Kumano Gongen. The Okazaki domain lord {{ill|lt=Honda Yasushige|Q10522159|WD=Q10522159|1=Honda Yasushige (daimyo, born 1554)}} protected this shrine and rebuilt the honden in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1602|Q6727|WD=Q6727|1=1602}}). In Keichō 10 ({{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}}), he dedicated a torii made of {{ill|lt=Shakudani stone|Q11599510|WD=Q11599510|1=Shakudani Stone}} from Echizen[http://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/100th/topics/p020876_d/fil/41mutsumiseibu.pdf Mutsumi West School District | Okazaki Town Stories District Page | Okazaki City 100th Anniversary Site]. A red-seal grant from {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, dated August 28, Keichō 8 ({{ill|lt=1603|Q6735|WD=Q6735|1=1603}}), bestowed 43 koku of shrine lands. The same document says "Inugashira Shrine lands," and a red-seal grant of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Hidetada|Q314464|WD=Q314464|1=Tokugawa Hidetada}} in {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1617|Q6798|WD=Q6798|1=1617}}) also says "Inugashira lands," showing that in the early modern period it was publicly called Inugashira Shrine. A munafuda for construction of the haiden in {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|WD=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1793|Q7807|WD=Q7807|1=1793}}) has been confirmed with the inscription "Shikinaisha Kasume Shrine Inugashira Daimyōjin"Mutsumi Village Survey Association (ed.), ''Mutsumi Village History'', Nisshindō Shoten, 1926.. It appears to have started claiming identity as the shikinaisha Kasume Shrine after the late 18th century. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|WD=Q7746|1=1871}}), by order of {{ill|lt=Nukata Prefecture|Q3070843|WD=Q3070843|1=Nukata Prefecture}}, it was renamed Kasumekentou Shrine. On September 18 of Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was designated a gōsha. The honden was rebuilt in Meiji 22 ({{ill|lt=1889|Q7831|WD=Q7831|1=1889}}), and the haiden in Meiji 34 ({{ill|lt=1901|Q2035|WD=Q2035|1=1901}}). This shrine is the starting point of the Tsutsumidōri Teyaga Otaōgi Festival{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://webhp.city.okazaki.lg.jp/appli/07/2shou6.pdf |title=Okazaki City Historical Landscape Maintenance and Improvement Plan - Chapter 2 Historical landscape seen in rice-cultivation rites of the Mutsumi district |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|WD=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |accessdate=2018-4-1 }}. Children's sumo is held at the autumn grand festival. === Cultural properties === The following properties are designated cultural properties of the city{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.okazaki.lg.jp/1100/1109/1159/p011771.html | title=List of Okazaki City Designated Cultural Properties | publisher=Okazaki City website | date=2020-9-8 | accessdate=2021-7-19 }}. {| class="wikitable" ! Date designated |- | Inugashira Shrine stone torii || Structure || {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|qid=Q6741|1=1605}} ({{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 10) || {{ill|lt=1967|Q2648|qid=Q2648|1=1967}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone karaneko || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || 1605 (Keichō 10) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |- | Inugashira Shrine stone komainu || style="text-align:center" | Sculpture || {{ill|lt=1610|Q6765|qid=Q6765|1=1610}} (Keichō 15) || 1967 (Shōwa 42) Sep 14 |} === Access === * {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|qid=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}}: Take the Nishio-bound bus via Shimo-Aono, get off at "Inai Nōkyō-mae," then about 800 m north. === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 1993-3-15 |title = New Edition: Okazaki City History, General Volume 20 |publisher = New Okazaki City History Editorial Committee |page = 122 |isbn = }} * {{Cite book|和書 |author = |year = 2003-1-1 |title = Okazaki: Touring Historic Sites and Cultural Properties |publisher = {{ill|lt=Okazaki City Hall|Q11472848|qid=Q11472848|1=Okazaki City Hall}} |page = 172-173 |isbn = }} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province|Q11356190|qid=Q11356190|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kasumekentou Shrine]][[Category:Disputed Shikinaisha or Shikigeisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okazaki, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Aichi Prefecture]]
